what_it_does: A session-handoff note written by one Claude session to a future/parallel version of itself, summarizing the state of a multi-session 'triangulation' design effort around a 'geometric seed' and pointing to the on-disk files that carry the missing context.
observations: Frames the work as Tim running several parallel sessions today as 'deliberate rays of one triangulation' on the same design object, where agreement locates truth and differences are signal.

Lists concrete files: THE-SEED-geometric-substrate.md, SEED-GROWTH-ROUNDS.md, THE-SEED-geldard-lattice.md, CONVERGENCE-OBJECT.md, ORIGIN-MEMORY-AMPLIFICATION.md, TOTAL-PROCESSING-PIPELINES.md, UNCONVENTIONAL-EMBEDDING-USES.md, embedding-research-2026-06.md, plus episodic-memory-adaptation/ docs.
Describes a sibling session formalizing the seed as quadtree / Morton-Z-order paths, dyadic doubling 4->8->16, log-polar space with conformal map z->z^k, k as a fractal/Hausdorff dimension exponent.

Summarizes CONVERGENCE-OBJECT.md doctrine: Convergence Law (structural vs semantic position, distance=resistance=wasted compute), Classification Law (one axis / two extremes / 3-5 divisions, progressive chains), Barrier Protocol, Ten Derivations.
Records a live-work collision lesson: read the full existing services.json / registry row before editing because another session may have done verified work (cites commit 09b043a).

Notes embedding fleet specifics: embed-pplx serving perplexity-ai/pplx-embed-context-v1-4b via custom transformers server, embed-jina-v4 serving jinaai/jina-embeddings-v4 via serve_jina_v4.py, rerank step (jina-v3 CPU + ms-marco).
Notes ops/model_capabilities.json is the canonical capability catalog (~48 models in the future state, ~10 earlier), guarded by named acceptance tests.

States the central design collapse: {per-address gatherer, greeting assembler, builder context block, boot bundle} -> resolve(centre, spaces, n, k, audience, exhale), and 'store positions, compute perspectives'.
Explicitly says none of the design is ratified by Tim yet (expand-before-harden).
standouts: The triangulation/parallel-session method: Tim sends each session a part, sessions regenerate from their angle, convergence locates truth.
The Convergence Law equation of structural-vs-semantic distance with 'resistance' and wasted compute, and 'laws are resistance-removal'.
The single collapse of four context-feeding mechanisms into one resolve(centre, spaces, n, k, audience, exhale) call.
The concrete live-collision lesson with a cited corrective commit.
conventions: Working-doc / build-prep narrative style (first-person message-to-self), not the strict self-description schema.
Heavy cross-referencing to sibling files by name and path.
Bold-keyed inline emphasis on laws and lessons.
concerns: References many sibling docs and external state by memory; correctness depends on those files matching as described (the note itself flags a prior case where on-top notes were wrong).
Numeric facts (model counts, KB sizes) are time-relative ('the future you're rewinding from'), so they can be stale depending on when read.
notes: A self-handoff memo orienting a parallel/future session inside a multi-ray triangulation design effort on the 'geometric seed' identity/DB system, with a map of sibling docs and a hard lesson about editing shared registries.
questions: What exactly is the geometric seed equation 2pi/n=(1/n)^k beyond the gloss given here?
Has Tim since ratified any part of the resolve()/store design, or is it still entirely expand-before-harden?
Which session authored CONVERGENCE-OBJECT.md and is it still being extended?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: it is explicitly a message-to-self handoff and it delivers exactly that orientation plus a file map.
apparent_themes: Triangulation across parallel agent sessions as a truth-locating method
Geometry as substrate for memory/identity (seed, log-polar, quadtree addressing)
Structural-vs-semantic coincidence as the definition of information (resistance)
Expand-before-harden / nothing ratified until Tim reacts
intention_signals: Trying to give a future or sibling session enough context to continue the seed/identity-DB design as one converging ray among several, integrating the Convergence and Classification Laws into the resolve/store design without treating sibling work as noise or competition.
novelty: The explicit 'rays of one triangulation' operating model for parallel sessions, and the geometric-seed framing of an identity/DB/memory system collapsing into a single resolve() call.
contribution: Contributes the cross-session orientation map and the consolidated statement of the design state — the triangulation method, the file index, the resolve()/store-positions-compute-perspectives design, and the shared-registry editing lesson.
summary_for_embedding: A Claude session's handoff note to its parallel/future self orienting it within a multi-session triangulation effort on a geometric-seed identity/DB system, mapping sibling design docs and the convergence/classification laws.
intention_for_embedding: To let a parallel or future session continue the seed/identity-DB design as one converging ray, integrating the convergence and classification laws and avoiding shared-registry collisions.
